summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a superb 1978 second set – epic Eyes of the World and a flawless Playing > Wheel > Playing sandwich shine through crystal‑clear audio.
review: The transfer is praised for its pristine, high‑fidelity soundboard quality, with a well‑balanced mix that even highlights Bob Weir up in the front. Musically the show dazzles – Eyes of the World is called “inspired,” Estimated and the Playing > Wheel > Playing sequence are described as “sweet” and “intricate,” and the long Playing jam earns special kudos, despite a brief moment where Garcia steps offstage. Overall the performance is regarded as one of the top shows of 1978.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both the musical performance (standout jams, tight set flow) and the recording quality (crisp soundboard source). Minor criticisms are limited to a brief guitar distortion issue.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear mix with strong instrument separation; some reviewers note Jerry's guitar can be a bit hot/distorted, but overall the transfer is described as amazing, crisp and well‑balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The second set flows tightly with seamless transitions, recalling the precision of the ’74 era while delivering fresh, improvisational energy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is highlighted as ripping through Eyes and the long Playing jam; a few notes mention his tone being “hot” or slightly distorted but overall praised for inventive solo work.
